message:
feat(conformance): add session_ops executor stubs (bd-tewv)

Add execute_* handlers for session_ops family:
- getlogin: returns ptr null check result
- getlogin_r: calls ABI function with buffer
- getsid: calls ABI function, returns valid_sid or error
- setsid: stub (would create new session)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-b5a.1","title":"Deterministic E2E scenario manifest catalog (smoke/stress/fault/stability)","description":"Background:
- E2E coverage must be scenario-driven and deterministic, not ad-hoc shell scripts.

Scope:
- Define scenario manifest catalog covering smoke, stress, fault injection, and long-run stability.
- For each scenario, require strict/hardened expected outcomes and artifact capture rules.

Deliverables:
1) Scenario manifest format and validation rules.
2) Initial prioritized scenario set for critical subsystems.
3) Mapping from scenario to required artifacts and pass conditions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Manifest parser rejects under-specified scenarios.
- Every scenario has deterministic replay metadata.

Rationale:
- Provides reusable contract for ongoing system validation.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for manifest parser/validator.
- E2E dry run validating scenario catalog load.
- Logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, expected_outcome, artifact_policy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedLotus","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:13.038407783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:27:29.001473031Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:27:29.001449387Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic E2E scenario manifest catalog, validator, dry-run loader path, and validation tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b5a.1","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1069,"issue_id":"bd-b5a.1","author":"RedLotus","text":"Claimed by RedLotus. Execution plan: (1) add deterministic scenario manifest schema + parser/validator, (2) seed initial prioritized scenario catalog (smoke/stress/fault/stability) with strict+hardened expectations and replay metadata, (3) wire e2e dry-run loader/validator path + structured trace log fields (trace_id, scenario_id, mode, expected_outcome, artifact_policy), (4) add unit tests for manifest validation and a script-level dry-run check. Will post commands and artifacts before closing.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:18:17Z"},{"id":1070,"issue_id":"bd-b5a.1","author":"RedLotus","text":"Implemented deterministic scenario catalog + parser/validator and wired e2e dry-run. Added tests/conformance/e2e_scenario_manifest.v1.json, scripts/validate_e2e_manifest.py, e2e suite manifest enforcement + metadata logging (scenario_id/expected_outcome/artifact_policy), stability class support, and dry-run mode (--dry-run-manifest). Updated scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh checks and added harness tests: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs + crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_manifest_validation_test.rs. Verification run: python3 scripts/validate_e2e_manifest.py validate --manifest tests/conformance/e2e_scenario_manifest.v1.json; bash scripts/e2e_suite.sh --dry-run-manifest fault strict; bash scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh (PASS). Cargo harness test run blocked by existing workspace compile error in frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs:442 (missing raw::clone_thread_asm).","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:27:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-b5a.2","title":"Replay engine + strict/hardened pair-run comparator for deterministic E2E","description":"Background:
- Determinism requires explicit controls for seeds, environment, scheduling assumptions, and fixture versions.

Scope:
- Build replay engine that pins environment, seeds, fixture checksums, and execution ordering.
- Enforce paired strict/hardened execution where declared.

Deliverables:
1) Replay controller with seed/environment pinning.
2) Strict/hardened pair runner and result comparator.
3) Repro bundle generation for failed runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same scenario + seed reproduces the same outcome/artifacts.
- Paired-mode mismatches are explicit and explainable.

Rationale:
- Eliminates non-reproducible E2E failures and shortens debug loops.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for replay key generation and comparator logic.
- E2E determinism tests across repeated runs.
- Logs: trace_id, scenario_id, replay_key, mode_pair_result, drift_flags.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedLotus","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:13.146171998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:34:55.188912911Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:34:55.188894206Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic replay key/env pinning, strict-hardened pair comparator, mismatch reporting, and replay validation tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","replay","strict-hardened"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b5a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b5a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1071,"issue_id":"bd-b5a.2","author":"RedLotus","text":"Claimed by RedLotus. Plan: add replay-key controller (seed + manifest hash + scenario_id + mode), record pinned env fingerprint per case, compute strict/hardened pair comparison report with explicit mismatch drift flags, and emit mode_pair_result structured logs. Add unit tests for replay key generation/comparator behavior and wire check_e2e_suite validation for replay fields.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:27:58Z"},{"id":1072,"issue_id":"bd-b5a.2","author":"RedLotus","text":"Implemented replay controller + strict/hardened pair comparator in scripts/e2e_suite.sh. Added deterministic replay_key/env_fingerprint generation (seed + manifest hash + scenario + mode + timeout + label), case-level replay metadata logging, per-scenario pair comparison with drift_flags and mode_pair_result logs, and mode_pair_report.json artifact emission. Updated scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh to validate replay fields and pair report integrity. Expanded harness tests for deterministic replay-key stability and mode-pair report semantics in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs; parser validation tests remain in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_manifest_validation_test.rs. Verification: bash scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh (PASS); cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --no-default-features --test e2e_suite_test --test e2e_manifest_validation_test (PASS).","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:34:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-b5a.3","title":"E2E pack implementation + CI gating + flake quarantine policy","description":"Background:
- The suite is only useful if integrated into CI with anti-flake controls and clear triage paths.

Scope:
- Implement prioritized scenario packs (smoke/stress/fault/stability).
- Wire CI with deterministic retry policy, flake quarantine labeling, and artifact retention index.

Deliverables:
1) Scenario pack implementations with detailed expected outputs.
2) CI job topology and gating thresholds.
3) Flake quarantine policy and remediation workflow.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Clean checkout runs complete pack deterministically.
- Failures are diagnosable from stored artifacts alone.

Rationale:
- Converts E2E from informational runs into release-blocking evidence.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for flake classifier and retry policy.
- E2E self-test for CI wiring.
- Logs: trace_id, scenario_pack, retry_count, flake_score, artifact_refs, verdict.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedLotus","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:13.254922341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:08.673408367Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:08.673331463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","e2e","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b5a.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b5a.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1073,"issue_id":"bd-b5a.3","author":"RedLotus","text":"Claimed by RedLotus. Plan: (1) add deterministic scenario-pack catalog with explicit expected outputs + gating thresholds, (2) add CI runner with deterministic retry policy + flake classifier + quarantine report, (3) wire check script/CI to enforce artifact retention + policy checks, (4) add focused tests for flake classification and retry policy behavior. Will post commands/artifacts before close.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:46:37Z"},{"id":1074,"issue_id":"bd-b5a.3","author":"RedLotus","text":"Completed by RedLotus. Delivered deterministic retry + flake classifier + quarantine workflow + scenario-pack gating + CI wiring. Key files: scripts/e2e_suite.sh (retry/flake/quarantine logs + reports), scripts/e2e_flake_policy.py (policy engine), tests/conformance/test_e2e_flake_policy.py (unit tests), scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh (gate validates new fields/reports), scripts/ci.sh (gate wired), crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs (new schema/report assertions). Evidence: bash scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh PASS; python3 -m unittest tests/conformance/test_e2e_flake_policy.py -q PASS; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --no-default-features --test e2e_manifest_validation_test --test e2e_suite_test PASS (12 tests total).","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-blg","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Condvar semantics spec (spurious wakeups, timeout clocks, errno rules)","description":"Background:
- Condition variables are subtle (spurious wakeups, timeout clocks, predicate loops) and require precise semantics before implementation.

Goal:
- Produce clean-room condvar semantics spec including clock handling and spurious-wakeup contract.

Deliverables:
1) wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait semantics table.
2) CLOCK_REALTIME vs CLOCK_MONOTONIC timeout conversion rules.
3) Spurious wakeup policy and required caller predicate-loop guidance.
4) Error mapping and invalid-usage handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Spec is implementation-ready and self-contained.
- Deferred/unsupported semantics explicitly documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for timeout conversion helpers and semantic table consistency.
- Structured logs for spec-check tools and generated matrices.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.435304263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:28.070333386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:28.070263545Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","pthread","spec","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1075,"issue_id":"bd-blg","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed condvar semantics spec (bd-blg). Deliverables:

1) cond_contract.md: Full transition table for init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast with state model (Uninitialized/Idle/Waiting/Destroyed).

2) cond.rs expanded with executable clean-room contract: CondvarContractState enum, CondvarContractOp enum, CondvarContractOutcome struct, condvar_contract_transition() const fn, CondvarAttributeContract with process_shared deferred, valid_timespec_nsec validator, futex_condvar_contention_note(), spurious_wakeup_policy().

3) 41 unit tests pass covering: all state x operation transitions, attribute support/deferral, timespec validation, lifecycle scenarios, signal/broadcast semantics, and policy documentation.

4) Conformance fixture: tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_cond.json with 24 test cases covering all 6 operations, clock handling, null pointer checks, timeout semantics, mutex mismatch, spurious wakeup pattern, and lifecycle.

5) Spec explicitly addresses: CLOCK_REALTIME/MONOTONIC timeout conversion, spurious wakeup predicate-loop requirement, mutex association invariant (all concurrent waiters same mutex), futex requeue optimization for broadcast, and deferred features (process-shared).

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (41 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bpe6","title":"Performance: stdio calls 4500x slower due to membrane overhead","notes":"puts() baseline: 31ns/call, with LD_PRELOAD: 138us/call (4500x slower). Root cause: each stdio call goes through runtime_policy::decide(), healing checks, scan_c_str_len(), and observe(). This causes smoke test performance failures (>2x threshold). Need fast-path bypass for hot stdio functions or optimized policy decision path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:45:22.050436442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.265027499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.262467997Z","close_reason":"Implemented strict/default stdio write fast path; rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed; focused assertions pass, with pre-existing stdio_abi_test teardown SIGSEGV tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1415,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Implemented strict/default fast path for hot stdio writes in stdio_abi.rs (fputc/fputs/fwrite/puts) so they bypass runtime_policy::decide/observe outside hardened mode while preserving hardened policy behavior. Formatted and diff-check clean; holding focused rch test until active peer builds clear.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:11:59Z"},{"id":1416,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Verification: rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed. Focused test strict_hot_stdio_writes_skip_runtime_policy_gate reported all assertions ok, but stdio_abi_test binary still exits SIGSEGV after teardown; existing puts_calls_without_crash and fputc_fgetc_and_ungetc_behave_consistently show the same ok-then-SIGSEGV pattern, so this is the pre-existing stdio ABI harness teardown crash rather than this fast-path assertion failing.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bwj","title":"Kernel: Localization fixed-point chooser (implement module)","description":"Implement runtime_math::localization controller.

Requirements:
- Pure deterministic integer scoring.
- Small constant-time evaluation.

Deliverables:

- Tests for determinism and tie-breaking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:40.243090502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:40:17.410872063Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:40:17.410842016Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bwj","depends_on_id":"bd-15q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-bwj","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1076,"issue_id":"bd-bwj","author":"PinkMill","text":"Implementation complete in localization_chooser.rs (290 lines, 12 tests). Delivered alongside design (bd-15q). EWMA-smoothed 5-signal input, 5-arm Euler-weighted scoring, O(1) integer-only. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:40:17Z"}]}

Start with --version, then test actual build operations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:53.059194748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29.300141372Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:28.041737230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.2","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:53.059194748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1336,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"gcc/g++ --version now works. Actual compilation via gcc has a -plugin linker argument issue (ld gets wrong args). Tracked separately.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ta3b","title":"C.1: Implement gshadow line parser in frankenlibc-core","description":"## Goal
Add a gshadow line parser to frankenlibc-core, following the exact pattern of the existing /etc/passwd parser in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

## Reference: Existing Pattern
The passwd parser at pwd/mod.rs uses:
- `pub struct Passwd { pw_name: Vec<u8>, pw_passwd: Vec<u8>, ... }`
- `pub fn parse_passwd_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — splits on colons, validates field count
- `pub fn lookup_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — iterates lines, matches name

The gshadow parser should follow this EXACT pattern for consistency.

## gshadow File Format (/etc/gshadow)
Each line: `group_name:encrypted_password:administrators:members`
- group_name: non-empty, no colons (same as /etc/group name field)
- encrypted_password: may be empty (""), or "!" (locked), or "*" (no password)
- administrators: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no admins)
- members: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no members)

Example lines:
  root:*::
  shadow:!::
  sudo:!::alice,bob
  dev:x:alice:alice,bob,charlie

## Implementation

### File: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs (NEW FILE)
Add `pub mod gshadow;` to crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

### Struct
```rust
pub struct Gshadow {
    pub sg_namp: Vec<u8>,    // group name
    pub sg_passwd: Vec<u8>,  // encrypted password
    pub sg_adm: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated admin list
    pub sg_mem: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated member list
}
```

### Functions
```rust
pub fn parse_gshadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
pub fn lookup_gshadow_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
```

### Parsing Logic
1. Skip empty lines and lines starting with #
2. Split on b':' — must have exactly 4 fields
3. Field 0 (name) must be non-empty
4. Fields 1-3 may be empty
5. Return Gshadow with owned Vec<u8> copies

## Inline Tests (#[cfg(test)])
- parse_valid_full_line: `sudo:!:admin1,admin2:user1,user2` -> all 4 fields correct
- parse_minimal_line: `root:*::` -> password="*", admins="", members=""
- parse_empty_password: `grp:::` -> password is empty
- parse_locked_password: `grp:!::` -> password is "!"
- parse_with_admins_and_members: `dev:x:alice:alice,bob` -> admins="alice", members="alice,bob"
- reject_too_few_fields: `root:*:` (3 colons needed, only 2) -> None
- reject_empty_name: `:*::` -> None
- reject_comment_line: `# comment` -> None
- reject_empty_line: "" -> None
- lookup_finds_entry: content with 3 lines, lookup "sudo" finds correct one
- lookup_returns_none_for_missing: lookup "nonexistent" returns None
- lookup_is_case_sensitive: "ROOT" does not match "root"

## Acceptance Criteria
Parser handles all gshadow field combinations. 12 inline tests pass. cargo clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:37.066192744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.720091402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.719809839Z","close_reason":"Implemented gshadow parser in frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs. Struct Gshadow with sg_namp/sg_passwd/sg_adm/sg_mem fields. parse_gshadow_line() handles comments, blank lines, field validation. lookup_gshadow_by_name() scans content for case-sensitive name match. 14 inline tests pass covering: valid full/minimal lines, empty/locked password, admin+member lists, reject malformed, lookup hit/miss/case sensitivity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pwd","stub-elimination"]}
+{"id":"bd-tewv","title":"Conformance: add execute_* handlers for session_ops family (getlogin, getsid, setsid)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:49:29.754063583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:49:39.183668124Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-thm2","title":"Fix c11threads managed-attr/host-pthread init mismatch","description":"c11threads_abi::mtx_init was building a FrankenLibC-managed pthread mutexattr word and passing it into host pthread_mutex_init, which triggered a glibc __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion in c11threads_abi_test. Fix the C11 mutex/condvar init lane so C11 synchronization objects are initialized consistently on the native-managed path, then verify the focused C11 suite and workspace ABI checks via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:34:41.394889788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204943932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204703667Z","close_reason":"Resolved C11 mutex/condvar init mismatch by keeping C11 synchronization initialization on the native-managed lane; focused c11threads tests now pass and workspace tests advance to unrelated dlfcn failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1358,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented scoped native-mutex delegation helpers in pthread_abi and switched c11threads mutex/condvar init to use the native-managed lane. Verification: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_mtx_init_lock_unlock_destroy -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed (36/36); `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` completed without diffs.
